verse: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
note: God’s love for humanity is shown through Jesus Christ and the promise of eternal life.

verse: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
note: God is the Creator of everything and the source of all life.

verse: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
note: God can use every situation in life for the good of those who trust Him.

verse: But as for you, ye thought evil against me; but God meant it unto good.
note: God can turn difficult and painful situations into something good.

verse: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
note: Christ gives believers strength to endure challenges and fulfill His purpose.

verse: The Lord shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
note: God protects and fights for His people in times of trouble.

verse: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
note: Jesus invites weary people to find peace and rest in Him.

verse: Thou shalt have no other gods before me.
note: God desires to be first and above all things in our lives.

verse: Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.
note: Faith in Christ brings spiritual renewal and a transformed life.

verse: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
note: God calls people to treat others with love and kindness.

verse: That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.
note: Salvation comes through faith in Jesus Christ and openly confessing Him.

verse: The Lord bless thee, and keep thee.
note: God’s blessing and protection are upon His people.

verse: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith.
note: A life led by the Holy Spirit produces godly character and attitudes.

verse: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thine heart.
note: God wants wholehearted love and devotion from His people.

verse: For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God.
note: Salvation is a gift from God and cannot be earned by human effort.

verse: Be strong and of a good courage; fear not, nor be afraid of them.
note: God gives courage and strength in fearful situations.

verse: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
note: God lovingly cares for His people and wants them to trust Him with their worries.

verse: Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid.
note: God encourages His people to trust Him and remain courageous.

verse: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.
note: Believers are called to shine God’s truth and goodness before others.

verse: The Lord is with thee, thou mighty man of valour.
note: God sees potential and strength in those He calls.

verse: If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally.
note: God generously gives wisdom to those who sincerely seek Him.

verse: Whither thou goest, I will go.
note: Ruth’s loyalty reflects faithfulness and commitment.

verse: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
note: Faith means trusting God even when we cannot yet see the outcome.

verse: Man looketh on the outward appearance, but the Lord looketh on the heart.
note: God values the heart and character more than outward appearance.

verse: But God commendeth his love toward us, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
note: Jesus sacrificed Himself out of love even while humanity was still in sin.

verse: As for God, his way is perfect.
note: God’s ways are trustworthy and without error.

verse: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
note: Jesus declared Himself to be the only path to God and eternal life.

verse: Let your heart therefore be perfect with the Lord our God.
note: Believers are encouraged to stay faithful and devoted to God.

verse: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
note: Prayer replaces anxiety with trust and thanksgiving toward God.

verse: If my people, which are called by my name, shall humble themselves, and pray.
note: God promises healing and forgiveness to those who repent and seek Him.

verse: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
note: God empowers believers with courage, love, and self-control.

verse: Ezra had prepared his heart to seek the law of the Lord.
note: Seeking God sincerely leads to wisdom and spiritual growth.

verse: Rejoice evermore.
note: Believers are encouraged to live with continual joy in God.

verse: The joy of the Lord is your strength.
note: True strength comes from the joy found in God.

verse: But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.
note: Putting God first brings direction and provision in life.

verse: Who knoweth whether thou art come to the kingdom for such a time as this?
note: God places people in positions for meaningful purposes.

verse: And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.
note: God’s truth brings spiritual freedom and transformation.

verse: The Lord gave, and the Lord hath taken away; blessed be the name of the Lord.
note: Job remained faithful to God even during suffering.

verse: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind.
note: Believers should allow God to change their thinking and way of life.

verse: For I know that my redeemer liveth.
note: Job expressed confidence and hope in God’s redemption.

verse: And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men.
note: Every task should be done sincerely as an act of service to God.

verse: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
note: God lovingly guides, protects, and provides for His people.

verse: We love him, because he first loved us.
note: Our love for God is a response to His love for us.

verse: The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
note: God brings courage and removes fear from those who trust Him.

verse: For with God nothing shall be impossible.
note: God’s power has no limits and nothing is beyond His ability.

verse: O taste and see that the Lord is good.
note: Experiencing God personally reveals His goodness.

verse: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.
note: Jesus never changes and remains faithful forever.

verse: Be still, and know that I am God.
note: God calls people to trust Him and rest in His power.

verse: Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you.
note: God welcomes those who sincerely seek a closer relationship with Him.

verse: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
note: God’s Word gives guidance and direction for life.

verse: With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible.
note: God can accomplish what human strength and wisdom cannot.

verse: Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
note: Believers should rely on God rather than human wisdom.

verse: God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able.
note: God provides strength and a way through temptation.

verse: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.
note: True wisdom begins with reverence and respect for God.

verse: Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing.
note: Trusting God fills believers with hope, joy, and peace.

verse: Commit thy works unto the Lord, and thy thoughts shall be established.
note: Giving plans to God brings stability and direction.

verse: If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.
note: Faith opens the door to experiencing God’s power.

verse: To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven.
note: Life has seasons, and God’s timing is perfect.

verse: Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.
note: Believers need spiritual protection through God’s strength and truth.

verse: Fear God, and keep his commandments.
note: The purpose of life is to honor and obey God.

verse: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.
note: Jesus offers a full and meaningful life through Him.

verse: Many waters cannot quench love.
note: True love is strong, enduring, and powerful.

verse: My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.
note: God’s grace sustains believers even in their weakness.

verse: But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength.
note: Trusting God brings renewed strength and endurance.

verse: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart.
note: Loving God fully is the greatest commandment.

verse: Fear thou not; for I am with thee.
note: God comforts His people with His constant presence.

verse: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins.
note: God faithfully forgives those who sincerely repent.

verse: He was wounded for our transgressions.
note: This prophecy points to the suffering servant who bears sin.

verse: Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved.
note: Faith in Jesus Christ brings salvation.

verse: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the Lord.
note: God’s plans for His people are filled with hope and purpose.

verse: For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
note: Sin leads to death, but God offers eternal life through Jesus.

verse: Call unto me, and I will answer thee.
note: God invites people to seek Him through prayer.

verse: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find.
note: God encourages persistent prayer and seeking after Him.

verse: It is of the Lord's mercies that we are not consumed.
note: God’s mercy and compassion are renewed every day.

verse: Let all your things be done with charity.
note: Love should guide every action and relationship.

verse: A new heart also will I give you.
note: God promises spiritual renewal and transformation.

verse: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap.
note: Doing good faithfully will eventually bring spiritual reward.

verse: Our God whom we serve is able to deliver us.
note: Faith trusts in God’s power even in dangerous situations.

verse: In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
note: Jesus gives hope and victory even during hardships.

verse: Daniel was taken up out of the den, and no manner of hurt was found upon him.
note: God protected Daniel because of his faithfulness.

verse: Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.
note: Believers should focus on eternal and spiritual priorities.

verse: For I desired mercy, and not sacrifice.
note: God values compassion and sincere devotion over empty rituals.

verse: And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
note: The Golden Rule teaches kindness and fairness toward others.

verse: Turn unto the Lord your God: for he is gracious and merciful.
note: God welcomes repentance with mercy and forgiveness.

verse: Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith.
note: Jesus is the source and perfect example of faith.

verse: Let judgment run down as waters, and righteousness as a mighty stream.
note: God desires justice and righteousness among people.

verse: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus.
note: Those who belong to Christ are forgiven and free from condemnation.

verse: The day of the Lord is near upon all the heathen.
note: God’s justice and judgment will ultimately prevail.

verse: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works.
note: Good actions should reflect God’s goodness to the world.

verse: I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the Lord, and he heard me.
note: God hears prayers even in moments of desperation.

verse: But be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only.
note: Faith should be demonstrated through obedient actions.

verse: What doth the Lord require of thee, but to do justly, and to love mercy.
note: God calls His people to justice, mercy, and humility.

verse: Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree.
note: Jesus bore humanity’s sins through His sacrifice on the cross.

verse: The Lord is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble.
note: God is a refuge and protector during difficult times.

verse: And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
note: God gives supernatural peace to those who trust Him.

verse: The just shall live by his faith.
note: Faith is essential for living in relationship with God.

verse: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul.
note: God desires wholehearted devotion from His people.

verse: The Lord thy God in the midst of thee is mighty.
note: God is present with His people in love and power.

verse: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
note: True love is shown through sacrifice and selflessness.

verse: Consider your ways.
note: God calls people to examine their priorities and actions.

verse: Behold, I stand at the door, and knock.
note: Jesus invites people into a personal relationship with Him.

verse: Not by might, nor by power, but by my spirit, saith the Lord.
note: God’s work is accomplished through His Spirit, not human strength.

verse: The Lord is not slack concerning his promise.
note: God is faithful to His promises and patient with humanity.

verse: Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse.
note: God encourages faithfulness and promises provision in return.
